@property\n    def has_required_failure(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if any required source failed.\"\"\"\n        return len(self.failed) > 0\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass FetchResult:\n     return self.sources.get(source)\n\n    def get_successful_data(self) -> dict[FetchSource, dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get data from all successful sources.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            source: Each\n304\t        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n305\t        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n306\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n307\t        # the dispatch itself.\n308\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n309\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n310\t\n311\t        try:\n312\t            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n313\t            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n314\t                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n315\t            else:\n316\t                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n317\t                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n318\t        except Exception:\n319\t            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n320\t            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n321\t            raise\n322\t\n323\t        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n324\t        return result\n325\t\n326\t    async def _persist_timings(\n327\t        self,\n328\t        intent_id: str,\n329\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n330\t    ) -> None:\n331\t        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Segment the utterance into \"\n    \"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \"\n    \"intent objects.\"\n)\n\n\nclass IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\n    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None):\n        self.store = store\n        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Load the router segmentation prompt from disk (hot-reload, per call).\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self.prompt_path.read_text()\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt from {self.prompt_path}: {e}\")\n            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n\n    def _load_urgency_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load the urgency classification prompt from disk via the hot-reload manager.\n\n        prompts/urgency.md is a separately hot-reloadable artifact (registered in\n        src/components/hot_reload.py). Splicing it here keeps urgency guidance\n        editable independently of the segmentation prompt. Returns \"\" on failure\n        so the router still functions without urgency guidance.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n            return \"\"\n\n    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build the full system prompt: segmentation prompt + urgency rules.\"\"\"\n        system_prompt = self._load_router_prompt()\n        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n        if urgency_prompt:\n            system_prompt = (\n                f\"{system_prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n            )\n        return system_prompt\n\n    async def classify_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Classify an utterance into intents.\n\n        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            session_id: Session ID for context\n\n        Returns:\n            List of IntentClassification objects\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n\n        # Build user message with session context if available\n        store = await self._get_store()\n        session_context = \"\"\n        try:\n            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n            if session:\n                # Get recent intents for context\n                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n                if recent_intents:\n                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n                    for intent in recent_intents:\n                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n\n        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n\n        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n        # Build system prompt per call from prompts/router.md (+ urgency rules),\n        # so edits to either prompt take effect without a server restart.\n        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n\n        try:\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=2048,\n                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n            )\n\n            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n            raw = response.strip()\n            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n            # Parse JSON response\n            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n            return classifications\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n            # Fallback: return single status intent\n            return [\n                IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=IntentType.STATUS,\n                    utterance_fragment=utterance,\n                    confidence=0.5,\n                    reasoning=\"Classification failed, defaulting to status\",\n                )\n            ]\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n        \"\"\"\n        # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n        # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n        # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n        classify_start = time.monotonic()\n        classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n        router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n\n        routed_intents = []\n        for classification in classifications:\n            # Create intent ID\n            intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n            routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                intent_id=intent_id,\n                classification=classification,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n                router_ms=router_ms,\n            )\n            routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n        return routed_intents\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n        # the dispatch itself.\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n\n        try:\n            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n            else:\n                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n        except Exception:\n            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n            raise\n\n        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n        return result\n\n    async def _persist_timings(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(classification.intent_type)\n\n            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n            from ..registry import get_project\n            repo_path = None\n            ssh_target = None\n            host_alias = None\n            registry = get_registry()\n            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n                if entry:\n                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n                    host_alias = entry.host\n                    location = entry.display_path\n                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n                else:\n                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n\n            # YAML registry entry carries cluster/namespace/argocd_app — cluster\n            # drives ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n            project_cfg = (\n                get_project(classification.project_slug)\n                if classification.project_slug else None\n            )\n\n            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                context=FetchContext(\n                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    repo_path=repo_path,\n                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n                    host_alias=host_alias,\n                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n                ),\n            )\n\n            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n\n            def _on_first_source(_source, _result) -> None:\n                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_first_source)\n            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n                timings.record(\n                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n                )\n\n            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                urgency=classification.urgency,\n            )\n\n            synth_start = timings.clock()\n            synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n            timings.record(\"synthesize_total_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(synth_start))\n            # synthesize_first_token_ms is not measurable on the current\n            # call_simple path (no token stream) and is left NULL until the\n            # synthesize strand streams — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n\n            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n            store = get_store()\n            _topic_type_map = {\n                IntentType.ACTION: \"project\",\n                IntentType.TASK_PROFILE: \"project\",\n            }\n            topic_type = _topic_type_map.get(classification.intent_type, \"research\")\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                data=synthesize_result.data,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"resolved\",\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"data\": synthesize_result.data,\n                \"summary\": synthesize_result.summary,\n                \"urgency\": synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                \"coverage\": synthesize_result.coverage,\n                \"caveats\": synthesize_result.caveats,\n                \"message\": \"Intent synthesized successfully\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Failed to fetch or synthesize intent\",\n            }\n\n    def _map_intent_type(self, intent_type: IntentType) -> FetchIntentType:\n        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\"\"\"\n        # Map enum values by string\n        type_map = {\n            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n        }\n        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n\n    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n    ) -> dict | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n\n        Returns:\n            Fence context dict with bead_id, refusal_reason, refusal_count, fenced_at\n            if fenced, None otherwise.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            bead_watch = await store.get_bead_watch(bead_ref)\n\n            if not bead_watch:\n                return None\n\n            # Check if bead is fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at)\n            last_refusal_reason = bead_watch.get(\"last_refusal_reason\")\n            fenced_at = bead_watch.get(\"fenced_at\")\n            refusal_count = bead_watch.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n            if last_refusal_reason or fenced_at:\n                # Bead is fenced - extract fence context\n                return {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": last_refusal_reason or \"Fenced (no reason provided)\",\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": fenced_at,\n                }\n\n            return None\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking fence status for bead {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n            return None\n\n    async def _create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        fence_context: dict,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a stuck card when a fenced bead is detected during intent routing.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent\n            fence_context: Fence context from _check_fence_for_bead\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with stuck card details\n        \"\"\"\n        from datetime import datetime\n\n        bead_id = fence_context[\"bead_id\"]\n        refusal_reason = fence_context[\"refusal_reason\"]\n        refusal_count = fence_context[\"refusal_count\"]\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Creating stuck card for fenced bead {bead_id} \"\n            f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n\n            # Get or create topic for this stuck card\n            topic_type = \"project\" if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else \"research\"\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Fenced: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[routed_intent.classification.project_slug] if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Link intent to topic\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Create stuck result\n            summary = f\"Task stuck — needs your input\"\n            data = {\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                \"message\": f\"This task has been blocked after {refusal_count} refusals.\",\n                \"action_hint\": \"Review the bead and provide the missing information or context needed to proceed.\",\n                \"fence_detected_during\": \"intent_routing\",\n            }\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=summary,\n                data=data,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created stuck card {result_id} for fenced bead {bead_id}\")\n\n            # Broadcast task_stuck event via SSE\n            broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n            await broadcaster.broadcast(\n                SSEEvent(\n                    event_type=EventType.TASK_STUCK,\n                    data={\n                        \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                        \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                        \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                        \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                        \"session_id\": routed_intent.session_id,\n                        \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                        \"timestamp\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n                    },\n                    target_session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                )\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": routed_intent.classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"stuck\",\n                \"bead_id\":            \"message\": \"Fenced bead detected during intent routing\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create stuck card from fence: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": routed_intent.classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Failed to create stuck card from fence context\",\n            }\n\n    async def _escalate_to_bead(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate a task-profile intent to a NEEDLESS bead.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to escalate\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records escalate_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with pending card or stuck card if fence detected\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Step 1: Check for existing fenced beads in the session before escalating\n        # This detects fence events when last_refusal_reason is set on existing beads\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            fenced_beads = await store.get_fenced_beads_for_session(\n                routed_intent.session_id\n            )\n\n            # If there are fenced beads, extract fence context and create stuck card\n            if fenced_beads:\n                # Get the most recently fenced bead\n                most_recent_fenced = fenced_beads[0]\n                bead_ref = most_recent_fenced[\"bead_ref\"]\n                refusal_reason = most_recent_fenced.get(\"last_refusal_reason\", \"Fenced (no reason provided)\")\n                refusal_count = most_recent_fenced.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n                fence_context = {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": most_recent_fenced.get(\"fenced_at\"),\n                }\n\n                logger.info(\n                    f\"Detected fenced bead {bead_ref} for session {routed_intent.session_id} \"\n                    f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n                )\n\n                # Create stuck card with fence context\n                return await self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                    routed_intent, fence_context\n                )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking for fenced beads: {e}\")\n            # Continue to escalation if fence check fails\n\n        # Step 2: No fenced beads detected - proceed with escalation\n        # Build escalate request\n        escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n            intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            context={\n                \"reasoning\": classification.reasoning,\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n            },\n            metadata={\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n                \"confidence\": classification.confidence,\n            },\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Escalate to bead — escalate_ms budgets formulation + validation +\n            # bf create (task-profile dispatches only; NULL for hot-path rows).\n            esc_start = timings.clock()\n            result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n            timings.record(\"escalate_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(esc_start))\n\n            # Check if the created bead is immediately fenced (race condition:\n            # watcher may have fenced it between escalate request and now)\n            if result.bead_id:\n                fence_context = await self._check_fence_for_bead(result.bead_id)\n                if fence_context:\n                    logger.info(\n                        f\"Detected immediate fence for bead {result.bead_id} \"\n                        f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n                    )\n                    # Return stuck card instead of pending card\n                    return await self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                        routed_intent, fence_context\n                    )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"escalated\",\n                \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n                \"pending_card\": result.pending_card,\n                \"message\": f\"Escalated to bead {result.bead_id}\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Escalation failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Failed to escalate to bead\",\n            }\n\n\n# Global router instance\n_router: Optional[IntentRouter] = None\n\n\ndef get_router(store=None) -> idx_signals_session ON feedback_signals(session_id);\n173\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_type ON feedback_signals(signal_type);\n174\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_processed ON feedback_signals(processed);\n175\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_result ON feedback_signals(result_id);\n176\t\n177\t-- Per-stage latency capture for every dispatch (Latency Budget & Instrumentation).\n178\t-- Keyed by the intent *thread* id (routed_intent.intent_id — the same id the\n179\t-- fetch/synthesize/escalate strands and the results row use), NOT the\n180\t-- intents.id the caller mints in create_intent(). router_ms is shared across\n181\t-- every intent thread from the same utterance; escalate_ms is NULL for hot-path\n182\t-- dispatches; stt_ms/first_render_ms are client-reported and NULL when the\n183\t-- client never reports them. synthesize_first_token_ms is NULL until the\n184\t-- synthesize strand streams (the non-streaming call_simple path can't measure\n185\t-- first token separately) — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n186\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dispatch_timings (\n187\t    intent_id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n188\t    router_ms                 INTEGER,\n189\t    fetch_first_source_ms     INTEGER,\n190\t    fetch_total_ms            INTEGER,\n191\t    synthesize_first_token_ms INTEGER,\n192\t    synthesize_total_ms       INTEGER,\n193\t    escalate_ms               INTEGER,\n194\t    sse_emit_ms               INTEGER,\n195\t    stt_ms                    INTEGER,\n196\t    first_render_ms           INTEGER,\n197\t    created_at                INTEGER NOT NULL\n198\t);\n199\t\n200\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dispatch_timings_created ON dispatch_timings(created_at);\n201\t\n202\t-- Bead watch table: circuit breaker tracking for async beads\n203\t-- Persistence layer for the async-path circuit breaker (plan §10 The Async Path).\n204\t-- Tracks open beads watched by the BeadWatcher, recording refusal counts,\n205\t-- last refusal reasons, SLA deadlines, and fencing state. All state is persisted\n206\t-- so a watcher restart loses nothing -- breaker state lives in the database,\n207\t-- not only in memory.\n208\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bead_watch (\n209\t    bead_ref           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,  -- References bead ID (intents.bead_ref)\n210\t    refusal_count      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- Number of REFUSED: comments seen\n211\t    last_refusal_reason TEXT,  -- Most recent refusal reason\n212\t    last_refusal_at    INTEGER,  -- Timestamp of most recent refusal\n213\t    comment_high_water INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT -1,  -- Latest comment index processed (-1 = none)\n214\t    sla_deadline       INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- Unix timestamp when SLA expires\n215\t    sla_flagged_at     INTEGER,  -- Timestamp when SLA was flagged (NULL if not flagged)\n216\t    fenced_at          INTEGER,  -- Timestamp when bead was fenced to status=blocked (NULL if not fenced)\n217\t    created_at         INTEGER NOT NULL  -- When this watch row was created\n218\t);\n219\t\n220\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline ON bead_watch(sla_deadline);\n221\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_watch_fenced ON bead_watch(fenced_at);\n222\t\"\"\"\n223\t\n224\t# The set of dispatch_timings timing columns record_dispatch_timings() may set.\n225\t# intent_id/created_at are handled explicitly (PK / insert-only timestamp).\n226\tDISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS = (\n227\t    \"router_ms\",\n228\t    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n229\t    \"fetch_total_ms\",\n230\t    \"synthesize_first_token_ms\",\n231\t    \"synthesize_total_ms\",\n232\t    \"escalate_ms\",\n233\t    \"sse_emit_ms\",\n234\t    \"stt_ms\",\n235\t    \"first_render_ms\",\n236\t)\n237\t\n238\t# Valid intent status values (plan §10 The Async Path: stuck/failed added for circuit breaker)\n239\tINTENT_STATUSES = ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')\n240\t\n241\t# Default SLA hours per intent type (plan §10 The Async Path: Visible Aging)\n242\t# task-profile: 6h (async bead-backed tasks)\n243\t# hot-path intents: 30s (budget is 3s, flag at 10x for aged pending)\n244\tDEFAULT_SLA_HOURS: dict[str, float] = {\n245\t    \"task-profile\": 6.0,  # 6 hours for async bead-backed tasks\n246\t    \"status\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path status intents\n247\t    \"action\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path action intents\n248\t    \"lookup\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path lookup intents\n249\t    \"brainstorm\": 0.5,   # 30 minutes for brainstorm (may need user iteration)\n250\t    \"reminder\": 24.0,    # 24 hours for reminders\n251\t}\n252\t\n253\t# Circuit breaker thresholds (plan §10 The Async Path)\n254\tCIRCUIT_BREAKER_REFUSAL_THRESHOLD = 3  # Fence after N refusals\n255\tCIRCUIT_BREAKER_AGE_THRESHOLD_HOURS = 24.0  # Fence after N hours without progress\n256\t\n257\t\n258\tclass SessionStore:\n259\t    \"\"\"Session store with WAL mode for concurrent access.\"\"\"\n260\t\n261\t    def __init__(self, db_path: Path):\n262\t        self.db_path = db_path\n263\t\n264\t    async def initialize(self) -> None:\n265\t        \"\"\"Initialize database with schema and WAL mode.\"\"\"\n266\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n267\t            # Enable WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes\n268\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n269\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n270\t\n271\t            # Create schema\n272\t            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n273\t            await db.commit()\n274\t\n275\t            # Additive migrations: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS above creates new\n276\t            # columns only for freshly-made DBs; an existing data/session.db\n277\t            # (e.g. the live Hetzner box) keeps its old column set and must be\n278\t            # ALTERed. Each statement is guarded by a PRAGMA table_info probe so\n279\t            # it is idempotent and never re-runs once the column exists.\n280\t            await self._migrate_additive_columns(db)\n281\t\n282\t    @staticmethod\n283\t    async def _migrate_additive_columns(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n284\t        \"\"\"Idempotently add columns introduced after the initial schema.\"\"\"\n285\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(results)\") as cur:\n286\t            result_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n287\t        if \"result_type\" not in result_cols:\n288\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN result_type TEXT\")\n289\t        if \"card_fallback\" not in result_cols:\n290\t            await db.execute(\n291\t                \"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0\"\n292\t            )\n293\t\n294\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n295\t            intent_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n296\t        if \"lookup_kind\" not in intent_cols:\n297\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents ADD COLUMN lookup_kind TEXT\")\n298\t\n299\t        # Migrate intents.status CHECK constraint to include 'stuck' and 'failed'\n300\t        # SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we need to recreate the table\n301\t        # First, check if the table still has the old constraint (without stuck/failed)\n302\t        await SessionStore._migrate_intents_status_enum(db)\n303\t\n304\t        # Migrate results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\n305\t        await SessionStore._migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db)\n306\t\n307\t        # Migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1\n308\t        await SessionStore._migrate_bead_watch_comment_high_water(db)\n309\t\n310\t        await db.commit()\n311\t\n312\t    @staticmethod\n313\t    async def _migrate_intents_status_enum(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n314\t        \"\"\"Migrate intents table to support 'stuck' and 'failed' status values.\n315\t\n316\t        Plan §10 The Async Path: extend intents status enum with stuck/failed.\n317\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we recreate the table.\n318\t        \"\"\"\n319\t        # Check if migration is needed by looking for 'stuck' in the schema\n320\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n321\t            cols = await cur.fetchall()\n322\t            status_col = None\n323\t            for col in cols:\n324\t                if col[1] == \"status\":\n325\t                    status_col = col\n326\t                    break\n327\t\n328\t        # If no status column or already has stuck/failed, skip migration\n329\t        if not status_col:\n330\t            return\n331\t\n332\t        # Check if the CHECK constraint already includes stuck/failed\n333\t        # by inspecting the SQL for the intents table\n334\t        async with db.execute(\n335\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='intents'\"\n336\t        ) as cur:\n337\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n338\t            if table_sql and (\"'stuck'\" in table_sql[0] or \"'failed'\" in table_sql[0]):\n339\t                # Already migrated\n340\t                return\n341\t\n342\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with new constraint\n343\t        logger.info(\"Migrating intents.status to include 'stuck' and 'failed' values\")\n344\t\n345\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n346\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n347\t\n348\t        try:\n349\t            # Create new table with updated constraint\n350\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n351\t                CREATE TABLE intents_new (\n352\t                    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n353\t                    utterance_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n354\t                    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n355\t                    topic_id     TEXT,\n356\t                    project_slug TEXT,\n357\t                    intent_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n358\t                    lookup_kind  TEXT,\n359\t                    status       TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')) DEFAULT 'pending',\n360\t                    bead_ref     TEXT,\n361\t                    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n362\t                    resolved_at  INTEGER,\n363\t                    FOREIGN KEY (utterance_id) REFERENCES utterances(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n364\t                    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n365\t                    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n366\t                )\n367\t            \"\"\")\n368\t\n369\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n370\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n371\t                INSERT INTO intents_new\n372\t                SELECT id, utterance_id, session_id, topic_id, project_slug,\n373\t                       intent_type, lookup_kind, status, bead_ref, created_at, resolved_at\n374\t                FROM intents\n375\t            \"\"\")\n376\t\n377\t            # Recreate indexes\n378\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_session\")\n379\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_topic\")\n380\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_status\")\n381\t\n382\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_session ON intents_new(session_id)\")\n383\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_topic ON intents_new(topic_id)\")\n384\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_status ON intents_new(status)\")\n385\t\n386\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n387\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE intents\")\n388\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents_new RENAME TO intents\")\n389\t\n390\t            await db.commit()\n391\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: intents.status now supports 'stuck' and 'failed'\")\n392\t\n393\t        except Exception as e:\n394\t            await db.rollback()\n395\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate intents.status: {e}\")\n396\t            raise\n397\t\n398\t    @staticmethod\n399\t    async def _migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n400\t        \"\"\"Migrate results table to allow NULL intent_id for monitoring-originated results.\n401\t\n402\t        Plan §10 Bead Watcher: monitoring-originated results have intent_id=NULL.\n403\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN to drop NOT NULL, so we recreate the table.\n404\t        \"\"\"\n405\t        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the results table schema\n406\t        async with db.execute(\n407\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='results'\"\n408\t        ) as cur:\n409\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n410\t            if not table_sql:\n411\t                return  # Table doesn't exist yet\n412\t\n413\t            # If intent_id is already nullable (NOT NULL not present), skip migration\n414\t            if \"intent_id   TEXT,\" in table_sql[0] or \"intent_id TEXT,\" in table_sql[0]:\n415\t                if \"NOT NULL\" not in table_sql[0].split(\"intent_id\")[1].split(\",\")[0]:\n416\t                    # Already migrated\n417\t                    return\n418\t\n419\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with nullable intent_id\n420\t        logger.info(\"Migrating results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\")\n421\t\n422\t        # Copy data from old table to new table\n452\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n453\t                INSERT INTO results_new\n454\t                SELECT * FROM results\n455\t            \"\"\")\n456\t\n457\t            # Recreate indexes\n458\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_session\")\n459\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_topic\")\n460\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_created\")\n461\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_previous\")\n462\t\n463\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_session ON results_new(session_id)\")\n464\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_topic ON results_new(topic_id)\")\n465\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_created ON results_new(created_at)\")\n466\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_previous ON results_new(previous_result_id)\")\n467\t\n468\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n469\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE results\")\n470\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results_new RENAME TO results\")\n471\t\n472\t            await db.commit()\n473\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: results.intent_id now allows NULL\")\n474\t        except Exception as e:\n475\t            await db.rollback()\n476\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate results.intent_id: {e}\")\n477\t            raise\n478\t\n479\t    @staticmethod\n480\t    async def _migrate_bead_watch_comment_high_water(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n481\t        \"\"\"Migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1.\n482\t\n483\t        The initial schema had DEFAULT 0, which meant the first comment (index 0)\n484\t        was skipped on the first check. With DEFAULT -1, no comments are skipped\n485\t        initially, and only comments with index > comment_high_water are processed.\n486\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN DEFAULT, so we recreate the table.\n487\t        \"\"\"\n488\t        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the bead_watch table schema\n489\t        async with db.execute(\n490\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='bead_watch'\"\n491\t        ) as cur:\n492\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n493\t            if not table_sql:\n494\t                return  # Table doesn't exist yet\n495\t\n496\t            # If comment_high_water already has DEFAULT -1, skip migration\n497\t            if \"DEFAULT -1\" in table_sql[0]:\n498\t                # Already migrated\n499\t                return\n500\t\n501\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with new default\n502\t        logger.info(\"Migrating bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1\")\n503\t\n504\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n505\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n506\t\n507\t        try:\n508\t            # Create new table with DEFAULT -1\n509\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n510\t                CREATE TABLE bead_watch_new (\n511\t                    bead_ref           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n512\t                    refusal_count      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n513\t                    last_refusal_reason TEXT,\n514\t                    last_refusal_at    INTEGER,\n515\t                    comment_high_water INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT -1,\n516\t                    sla_deadline       INTEGER NOT NULL,\n517\t                    sla_flagged_at     INTEGER,\n518\t                    fenced_at          INTEGER,\n519\t                    created_at         INTEGER NOT NULL\n520\t                )\n521\t            \"\"\")\n522\t\n523\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n524\t            # Existing rows with comment_high_water=0 are migrated as-is\n525\t            # (they represent \"comment 0 has been processed\", which is valid)\n526\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n527\t                INSERT INTO bead_watch_new\n528\t                SELECT * FROM bead_watch\n529\t            \"\"\")\n530\t\n531\t            # Recreate indexes\n532\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline\")\n533\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_bead_watch_fenced\")\n534\t\n535\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline ON bead_watch_new(sla_deadline)\")\n536\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_bead_watch_fenced ON bead_watch_new(fenced_at)\")\n537\t\n538\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n539\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE bead_watch\")\n540\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE bead_watch_new RENAME TO bead_watch\")\n541\t\n542\t            await db.commit()\n543\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: bead_watch.comment_high_water default is now -1\")\n544\t        except Exception as e:\n545\t            await db.rollback()\n546\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water: {e}\")\n547\t            raise\n548\t\n549\t    async def close(self) -> None:\n550\t        \"\"\"Close database connection pool.\"\"\"\n551\t        # aiosqlite uses connection-per-context, so just ensure checkpoint\n552\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n553\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)\")\n554\t\n555\t    # Session operations\n556\t    async def create_session(self, session_id: str | None = None) -> str:\n557\t        \"\"\"Create a new session and return its ID.\"\"\"\n558\t        if not session_id:\n559\t            session_id = str(uuid4())\n560\t        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n561\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n562\t            await db.execute(\n563\t                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO sessions (id, created_at, last_active) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\",\n564\t                (session_id, now, now)\n565\t            )\n566\t            await db.commit()\n567\t        return session_id\n568\t\n569\t    async def get_session(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n570\t        \"\"\"Get session by ID.\"\"\"\n571\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n572\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n573\t            async with db.execute(\n574\t                \"SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n575\t            ) as cursor:\n576\t                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n577\t                return dict(row) if row else None\n578\t\n579\t    async def update_session_activity(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n580\t        \"\"\"Update session last_active timestamp.\"\"\"\n581\t        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n582\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n583\t            await db.execute(\n584\t                \"UPDATE sessions SET last_active = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n585\t                (now, session_id)\n586\t            )\n587\t            await db.commit()\n588\t\n589\t    async def set_primary_surface(self, session_id: str, surface_id: str) -> None:\n590\t        \"\"\"Set the primary surface for a session.\"\"\"\n591\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n592\t            await db.execute(\n593\t                \"UPDATE sessions SET primary_surface_id = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n594\t                (surface_id, session_id)\n595\t            )\n596\t            await db.commit()\n597\t\n598\t    async def delete_session(self, session_id: str) -> dict:\n599\t        \"\"\"Delete a session and every row tied to it. Returns a removal summary.\n600\t\n601\t        Used by test teardown (DELETE /api/v1/sessions/{id}) to guarantee a\n602\t        session and all its topics/results/intents/utterances/surfaces/signal\n603\t        rows are gone. Note: SQLite FK ``ON DELETE CASCADE`` is *not* enforced\n604\t        here (``PRAGMA foreign_keys`` is never enabled), so child rows are\n605\t        deleted explicitly, in dependency order, within one transaction.\n606\t\n607\t        The topics table references sessions with ``ON DELETE SET NULL``, so\n608\t        topics are *not* cascade-removed even if FKs were on — deleting them\n609\t        explicitly is required for a true clean teardown.\n610\t\n611\t        Returns ``{\"session_removed\": 0|1, \"topics_removed\": <int>}``.\n612\t        \"\"\"\n613\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n614\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n615\t\n616\t            # Count what we're about to remove (before deleting).\n617\t            async with db.execute(\n618\t                \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM topics WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n619\t            ) as cur:\n620\t                topics_removed = (await cur.fetchone())[0]\n621\t            async with db.execute(\n622\t                \"SELECT 1 FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n623\t            ) as cur:\n624\t                session_removed = 1 if await cur.fetchone() else 0\n625\t\n626\t            # Children first, parents last (matches FK dependency order).\n627\t            await db.execute(\n628\t                \"DELETE FROM feedback_signals WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n629\t            )\n630\t            await db.execute(\n631\t                \"DELETE FROM results WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n632\t            )\n633\t            await db.execute(\n634\t                \"DELETE FROM intent_topics WHERE intent_id IN \"\n635\t                \"(SELECT id FROM intents WHERE session_id = ?)\",\n636\t                (session_id,),\n637\t            )\n638\t            # dispatch_timings is keyed by the intent *thread* id\n639\t            # (routed_intent.intent_id), which differs from intents.id for the\n640\t            # current router path (see the table comment in SCHEMA_SQL), so this\n641\t            # is best-effort: it catches rows keyed by the store intent id, while\n642\t            # thread-keyd rows survive until the thread id is known to the caller.\n643\t            await db.execute(\n644\t                \"DELETE FROM dispatch_timings WHERE intent_id IN \"\n645\t                \"(SELECT id FROM intents WHERE session_id = ?)\",\n646\t                (session_id,),\n647\t            )\n648\t            await db.execute(\n649\t (session_id,)\n653\t            )\n654\t            await db.execute(\n655\t                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE topic_id IN \"\n656\t                \"(SELECT id FROM topics WHERE
